title: Complete local-agent installer adaptation
goal: Make Better Plan installation and verification cover the local Windows and WSL agent runtimes without duplicate implementations or unverifiable adapters.
description: One platform-neutral installer plan covering WSL OpenCode deployment, runtime-aware verification, and the Claude plugin manifest.

title: Enforce node execution with lifecycle hooks
goal: Make platform eligibility and post-node regression deterministic across Better Plan's state machine and Codex, Cursor, and Claude Code lifecycle hooks.
description: One enforcement plan covering a machine-readable regression contract, a platform-aware node gate, lifecycle adapters, scoped installation, and end-to-end validation.

title: Constrain lifecycle hooks and harden privacy
goal: Make Better Plan Hooks automatically project-detected, repository-scoped, bounded, privacy-safe, and unable to deadlock unrelated agent work.
description: A corrective maintenance plan grounded in the observed cross-project Cursor deadlock and the repository privacy audit.

title: Align submitted tasks with current plans
goal: Detect Better Plan projects at session entry and keep every submitted task semantically aligned with the current nonterminal Plan and Nodes without intercepting tools.
description: A lifecycle-context plan covering structural project detection, latest-intent alignment, safe Plan correction, host protocol differences, and regression verification.

title: Orchestrate Better Plan delivery with fresh subagents
goal: Make the main agent orchestrate plan alignment, independent acceptance design, sequential Node execution, and independent stop-time audit through fresh subagents.
description: A workflow orchestration plan that assigns planning, execution, and acceptance review to isolated agents while preserving Better Plan state-machine and privacy boundaries.

title: Automate acceptance-driven Node transitions
goal: Make predesigned tests and independent audit verdicts drive a predictable implementation, repair, verification, and completion state machine.
description: A follow-up workflow plan that replaces agent-managed acceptance checkboxes with deterministic post-executor regression, repair routing, audit routing, and receipt-bound automatic completion.

title: Remove obsolete CLI test vocabulary
goal: Remove the final permanent deleted-syntax test and revalidate the current-only Better Plan interface.
description: A bounded follow-up plan that preserves current criterion-replacement coverage, verifies deleted syntax once, and records a fresh final receipt without rewriting completed Plan history.

title: Split Better Plan into explicit architecture layers
goal: Replace monolithic workflow and installer scripts with typed domain, application, infrastructure, and adapter modules behind thin executable entrypoints.
description: A complete internal architecture migration that preserves current CLI behavior, removes old module implementations, introduces event-driven Agent-completion boundaries, and keeps the Better Plan skill concise and installable.

title: Keep Better Plan delivery lightweight
goal: Run one bounded Better Plan lifecycle for one user-visible capability while keeping acceptance selection and waiting behavior risk-based and main-controlled.
description: A generic lightweight workflow correction that prevents automatic capability expansion, removes mechanical acceptance-category coverage, and returns every revision or new-scope decision to the native main.

title: Keep the test suite portable on Windows
goal: Make the complete Better Plan regression suite deterministic on Windows with Python 3.10 without weakening the documented CLI contract.
description: A bounded portability correction for test argument ordering, version-gated AST nodes, external executable isolation, and Windows CI coverage.

title: Maintain the current local-agent target matrix
goal: Replace retired Gemini maintenance with native Better Plan installation for Antigravity, Pi, and Craft Agents.
description: One installer-matrix migration that removes the Gemini target completely and adds bounded, native integrations for Antigravity, Pi, and every configured Craft workspace.

title: Support Better Plan in Kimi Code
goal: Install, guide, advance, diagnose, and uninstall Better Plan through Kimi Code's native Skill and Hook contracts.
description: One bounded agent-target integration using Kimi Code's current user Skill discovery and lifecycle Hook configuration without tool interception or forced continuation.

title: Keep deferred goals reachable and dependency graphs acyclic
goal: Keep intentionally deferred goals visible and resumable while rejecting real dependency cycles across the complete workspace.
description: One planning-reachability correction that separates terminal waiver from future scheduling and makes every Node wait part of one workspace-wide dependency graph.
